Motion To Set Aside Payment Arrangements And Enter Judgment Against Defendant
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (U.S.A.) N.A.'s Motion To Set Aside Payment Arrangements And Enter Judgment Against Defendant.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to set aside payment arrangements and enter judgment against Defendant is granted in part and denied in part. The Stipulation for Judgment provides that if Defendant does not make a total settlement payment of $1,970.00, judgment may be entered in the amount of $3,216.84, plus costs in the amount of $325.00. As such, it is an unenforceable penalty or liquidated damages provision because it bears no reasonable relationship to the range of actual damages the parties could have anticipated from Defendant's failure timely to pay the agreed upon $1,970 settlement amount. (See, e.g., Graylee v. Castro (2020) 52 Cal.App.5th 1107, 1113-1119; Vitatech Internat., Inc. v. Sporn (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 796, 805-814; Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495, 500.) If Plaintiff so elects, the Court will enter judgment in Plaintiff's favor in the amount of $1,970.00.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and a separate proposed judgment.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-04-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PHILIP M. GREEN, Esq. of Open Door Legal (Tel: 628-203-8494) as movant/attorney-of-record for defendant Rodney James;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing re: Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Re: Defendant Rodney James.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

Argument presented by Mr. Green. The Court rules as follows: Mr. Green's Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is granted on the condition that Mr. Green makes another attempt to communicate with Mr. James regarding the October 26, 2020 trial date set in this case. Mr. Green is then to submit a proposed order using and completing the mandatory judicial council form and email that proposed order to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
